volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 485 - Section 680)

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: κείνου τε καὶ σὴν ἐξ ἴσου κοινὴν χάριν
sections.translation: for his sake and for yours equally bear with the woman , and be willing that the gentle words which you spoke about her have been spoken unalterably . For though by the strength of his hands he is victorious in all else , Heracles has been utterly subdued by his passion for this girl .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὧδε καὶ φρονοῦμεν ὥστε ταῦτα δρᾶν ,
sections.translation: My own thoughts move me to act as you advise . Rest assured that I will not earn another affliction for myself by waging a fruitless fight against the gods . But let us go into the house , so that you may carry him my messages and since gifts should be given in due recompense for gifts received ,

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἄγῃς · κενὸν γὰρ οὐ δίκαιά σε
sections.translation: so that you may take these also . It would not be right that you should go back with empty hands , after coming with such a rich cargo . Exit Lichas , the Messenger and Deianeira into the house .

sections.label: Section 1.497
sections.original: μέγα τι σθένος ἁ Κύπρις ἐκφέρεται νίκας ἀεί .
sections.translation: Great and mighty is the victory which the Cyprian queen always bears away . I bypass the tales of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1.500
sections.original: παρέβαν , καὶ ὅπως Κρονίδαν ἀπάτασεν οὐ λέγω ,
sections.translation: and do not narrate how she beguiled the son of Cronus , and Hades , the lord of darkness , or Poseidon , shaker of the earth . But , when this bride was to be won ,

sections.label: Section 1.505
sections.original: τίνες πάμπληκτα παγκόνιτά τʼ ἐξῆλθον ἄεθλʼ ἀγώνων ;
sections.translation: who were the massive rivals that entered the contest for her nuptials ? Who stepped forward to the ordeal of battle full of blows and raising dust ?

sections.label: Section 1.507
sections.original: ὁ μὲν ἦν ποταμοῦ σθένος , ὑψίκερω τετραόρου
sections.translation: One was a mighty river-god , the form of a bull , high-horned and four-legged ,

sections.label: Section 1.510
sections.original: Ἀχελῷος ἀπʼ Οἰνιαδᾶν , ὁ δὲ Βακχίας ἀπὸ
sections.translation: Achelous , from Oeniadae . The other came from Thebes , home of Bacchus , brandishing his resilient bow , his spears and club ; he was the son of Zeus . These two then met in a mass , lusting to win a bride ,

sections.label: Section 1.515
sections.original: μόνα δʼ εὔλεκτρος ἐν μέσῳ Κύπρις ῥαβδονόμει ξυνοῦσα .
sections.translation: and the Cyprian goddess of nuptial joy was there with them , acting as sole umpire .

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: τότʼ ἦν χερός , ἦν δὲ τόξων πάταγος ,
sections.translation: There was clatter of fists and clang of bow and crash of a bull ’ s horns mixed together ;

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἦν δʼ ἀμφίπλεκτοι κλίμακες ,
sections.translation: then there were close-locked grapplings and deadly blows from foreheads and loud deep cries from both . Meanwhile the delicate beauty sat on the side of a hill that could be seen from afar ,

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: ἧστο , τὸν ὃν προσμένουσʼ ἀκοίταν .
sections.translation:
awaiting the husband that would be hers .
So the battle rages , as I narrate . But the face of the bride which is the prize of the strife awaits the end in piteous anguish . And suddenly she has left her mother ,

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ὥστε πόρτις ἐρήμα .
sections.translation: like an orphaned calf .

sections.label: Section 531
sections.original: ἦμος , φίλαι , κατʼ οἶκον ὁ ξένος θροεῖ
sections.translation: Dear friends , while our guest is saying his farewell to the captive girls in the house , I have stolen away partly to tell you what these hands have devised ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: τὰ δʼ οἷα πάσχω συγκατοικτιουμένη .
sections.translation:
and partly to grieve over my sufferings in your company .
I have received a maiden — or , I believe , no longer a maiden , but an experienced woman — into my home , just as a mariner takes on cargo , a merchandise to wreck my peace of mind . And now we are two , a pair waiting under

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: χλαίνης ὑπαγκάλισμα . τοιάδʼ Ἡρακλῆς ,
sections.translation: a single bedspread for one man ’ s embrace . Such is the reward that Heracles has sent me — he whom I called true and loyal — for guarding his home through all that long time . I do not know how to be angry with him , even though he is infected with this disease .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: τὸ δʼ αὖ ξυνοικεῖν τῇδʼ ὁμοῦ τίς ἂν γυνὴ
sections.translation: But , then again , to live with her , sharing the same marriage — what woman could endure it ? For I see that the flower of her youth is blossoming , while mine is fading . The eyes of men love to pluck off the bloom of youth , but they turn their steps from the old .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: ταῦτʼ οὖν φοβοῦμαι μὴ πόσις μὲν Ἡρακλῆς
sections.translation:
On this account I am afraid lest Heracles , in name my husband , should be the younger woman ’ s man .
But , as I said , anger brings shame to a woman of understanding . I will tell you , my friends , the way by which I will have deliverance and relief .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: ἦν μοι παλαιὸν δῶρον ἀρχαίου ποτὲ
sections.translation: I had a gift , an old one given to me by a monster of long ago , and kept it hidden in a bronze urn . While yet a girl , I took this gift from the shaggy-chested Nessus — from his lifeblood , as he lay dying . He is the one who used to carry men

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: μισθοῦ ʼπόρευε χερσίν , οὔτε πομπίμοις
sections.translation: in his arms for hire across the deep current of the Evenus , using no oar for conveyance , nor ship ’ s sail . He carried me , too , on his shoulders , when at my father ’ s sending , I first departed with Heracles as his wife . When I was in midstream ,

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ψαύει ματαίαις χερσίν · ἐκ δʼ ἤυσʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: he touched me with lewd hands . I shrieked , and straightaway the son of Zeus turned round and with his hands shot a feathered arrow that whistled right through his chest to the lungs . As he passed away the monster spoke these few words : “ Child of aged Oeneus ,

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: τοσόνδʼ ὀνήσει τῶν ἐμῶν , ἐὰν πίθῃ ,
sections.translation: you will have this benefit from my ferrying , if you obey me , since you were the last whom I carried . If you gather with your hands the blood clotted round my wound , at the place where the Hydra , Lerna ’ s monstrous growth , imbued the arrow with black gall ,

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: ἔσται φρενός σοι τοῦτο κηλητήριον
sections.translation:
you will have a charm for the heart of Heracles , so that he will never look upon any woman and love her more than you .”
Remembering this charm , my friends — for , after his death , I had kept it carefully locked up in the house —

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: χιτῶνα τόνδʼ ἔβαψα , προσβαλοῦσʼ ὅσα
sections.translation: I have imbued this robe with it , applying to it all that he instructed while he lived . The work is finished . May deeds of wicked daring always be far from my thoughts and from my knowledge , as I detest the women who attempt them ! But if in any way I may prevail against this girl by love-spells

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: τὴν παῖδα καὶ θέλκτροισι τοῖς ἐφʼ Ἡρακλεῖ ,
sections.translation: and the charms used on Heracles , the means to that end have been devised — unless I seem to be acting rashly . If so , I will stop immediately .

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴ τις ἐστὶ πίστις ἐν τοῖς δρωμένοις ,
sections.translation: On the contrary . If there is any reason for confidence in these actions , we think that your plan is not wrong .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: οὕτως ἔχει γʼ ἡ πίστις , ὡς τὸ μὲν δοκεῖν
sections.translation: My reason for confidence is this : there is the appearance of success in the plan . But the proof I have not yet essayed .

sections.label: Section 592
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰδέναι χρὴ δρῶσαν , ὡς οὐδʼ εἰ δοκεῖς
sections.translation: Your knowledge must come after you act . For you can have no test which is not fanciful except by making the attempt .

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὐτίκʼ εἰσόμεσθα , τόνδε γὰρ βλέπω
sections.translation: Well , we shall know shortly , for there I see the man

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: θυραῖον ἤδη · διὰ τάχους δʼ ἐλεύσεται .
sections.translation: already at the doors . He will soon depart . Only I ask that I may be well sheltered by you ! When you accomplish even shameful deeds under the cover of darkness , you will never fall into disgrace .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: τί χρὴ ποεῖν ; σήμαινε , τέκνον Οἰνέως ,
sections.translation: What are your instructions ? Tell me , daughter of Oeneus , for already I am tardy because of my long stay .

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὐτὰ δή σοι ταῦτα καὶ πράσσω , Λίχα ,
sections.translation: In fact I have just been seeing to this very thing for you , Lichas , while you were addressing the foreign women in the house : take for me this long robe , prepared by my own hand , a gift to my absent husband . Give it to him and tell him to be sure that no man

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: κείνου πάροιθεν ἀμφιδύσεται χροΐ ,
sections.translation: puts it on his body before him and that it not be seen by the light of the sun , nor by the sacred precinct , nor by the blaze at the hearth , until he stands conspicuous before all eyes and shows it clearly to the gods on a day for the sacrifice of bulls .

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: οὕτω γὰρ ηὔγμην , εἴ ποτʼ αὐτὸν ἐς δόμους
sections.translation: For so had I vowed , that if I should ever see or hear that he had come safely home , I would dutifully adorn him with this robe and reveal him to the god as a new sacrificer in new garb . As proof of your commission you will carry a token which he will easily recognize

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: σφραγῖδος ἕρκει τῷδʼ ἐπὸν μαθήσεται .
sections.translation:
within the circle of this seal .
Now go ; and first , observe the rule that messengers should be unwilling to act out of line , and second , see to it that my thanks may be joined to his , doubling the gratitude you earn .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴπερ Ἑρμοῦ τήνδε πομπεύω τέχνην
sections.translation: If I practice Hermes ’ art of heraldry with any skill , I certainly will never slip up while doing your errand . I will not fail to carry and present this chest in the same condition , nor to add to it the proper assurance of the reasons which you have for sending it .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: στείχοις ἂν ἤδη · καὶ γὰρ ἐξεπίστασαι
sections.translation: You may go now , for you know well

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: τά γʼ ἐν δόμοισιν ὡς ἔχοντα τυγχάνει .
sections.translation: how things are with us in the house .

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: ἐπίσταμαί τε καὶ φράσω σεσωσμένα .
sections.translation: I know and will report that all is well .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶσθα μὲν δὴ καὶ τὰ τῆς ξένης ὁρῶν
sections.translation: And you know , further , from your own eyes my greeting to the foreign girl , how I welcomed her warmly .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἐκπλαγῆναι τοὐμὸν ἡδονῇ κέαρ .
sections.translation: So much so that my heart was struck with joy .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν ἄλλο γʼ ἐννέποις ; δέδοικα γὰρ
sections.translation: What more , then , is there for you to tell him ? I am afraid that it would be too soon to speak of the longing on my part , before I know if I am longed for there . Exit Lichas on one side and Deianeira into the house .

sections.label: Section 1.633
sections.original: ὦ ναύλοχα καὶ πετραῖα
sections.translation: O you who dwell by the warm springs near ships ’ haven and rocky cliff , and you who live by

sections.label: Section 1.635
sections.original: Οἴτας παραναιετάοντες , οἵ τε μέσσαν Μηλίδα πὰρ λίμναν
sections.translation: the crags of Oeta ! And you dwellers by the land-locked waters of the Malian gulf , and the headland sacred to the virgin-goddess of the golden shuttle , where are the famed councils of the Greeks at the gates !

sections.label: Section 1.640
sections.original: ὁ καλλιβόας τάχʼ ὑμῖν
sections.translation: Soon the glorious voice of the flute will go up for you again , sounding no bothersome , strident notes , but divine music worthy of the lyre ! For the son whom Alcmena bore to Zeus

sections.label: Section 1.645
sections.original: σοῦται πάσας ἀρετᾶς
sections.translation: is hurrying homeward , with spoils won by supreme valor .

sections.label: Section 2.647
sections.original: ὃν ἀπόπτολιν εἴχομεν παντᾷ ,
sections.translation: We thought him lost utterly to our land , a wanderer over sea , while we waited through twelve long months and knew nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.650
sections.original: ἁ δέ οἱ φίλα δάμαρ
sections.translation: She , his loving wife , miserable , was ever pining in her miserable heart , always weeping . But now Ares , stung to rage , has freed her days from their toil .

sections.label: Section 2.655
sections.original: ἀφίκοιτʼ ἀφίκοιτο · μὴ σταίη
sections.translation: May he come , may he come ! Let him not halt the many-oared ship that carries him before he has reached this town , leaving the island altar where he is reported to be sacrificing !

sections.label: Section 2.660
sections.original: ὅθεν μόλοι πανίμερος ,
sections.translation: May he come from there full of desire , steeped in love on the pretext of the robe by Persuasion ’ s all-powerful ointment !

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ὡς δέδοικα μὴ περαιτέρω
sections.translation: Friends , how I fear that I may have gone too far in all that I was doing just now !

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι , Δῃάνειρα , τέκνον Οἰνέως ;
sections.translation: What has happened , Deianeira , daughter of Oeneus ?

sections.label: Section 666
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἀθυμῶ δʼ , εἰ φανήσομαι τάχα
sections.translation: I do not know , but I despair that I will soon be found to have done a great wrong , when my hope was for good .

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: οὐ δή τι τῶν σῶν Ἡρακλεῖ δωρημάτων ;
sections.translation: Surely it is nothing that bears on your gift to Heracles ?

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: μάλιστά γʼ , ὥστε μήποτʼ ἂν προθυμίαν
sections.translation: Yes , it is ! So much so that I would never recommend to anybody

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ἄδηλον ἔργου τῳ παραινέσαι λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: that he be eager for action in obscurity .

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: δίδαξον , εἰ διδακτόν , ἐξ ὅτου φοβεῖ .
sections.translation: Tell us the cause of your fear , if it may be told .

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: τοιοῦτον ἐκβέβηκεν οἷον , ἢν φράσω ,
sections.translation: A thing has happened , my friends , such that , if I declare it , it will be a strange marvel for you to hear . The implement with which I was just now anointing the festal robe ,

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ἔχριον , ἀργὴς οἰὸς εὐέρου πόκος ,
sections.translation: a white tuft of fleecy sheep ’ s wool , has disappeared , eaten away by no animal in the courtyard , but self-devoured and self-destroyed it crumbled down over a stone slab . But I must tell the story at greater length , so that you may know exactly how this thing was accomplished .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ἐγὼ γὰρ ὧν ὁ θήρ με Κένταυρος , πονῶν
sections.translation: I neglected no part of the precepts which the savage Centaur gave me when he was hurting from the bitter barb in his side ; they were in my memory , like the graven words which no hand may wash from a tablet of bronze . Now this was his order to me , and I obeyed it :
